import { Badge, Callout, Checklist, Figure, Tabs, Tab } from './guide-components.js'
import StatusMap from './status-map.jsx'
export const release = { name: 'Harbor Light', version: '4.8.0', window: '21:30 UTC' }
export const contacts = ['Mina', 'Osei', 'Ren']

# Harbor Observatory release guide

This is the working notebook for {release.name} **v{release.version}**. It keeps the
weather display, buoy importer, and public map moving together without turning a
routine evening release into a ceremony.

<Badge kind="release" label={release.version} />

The control room still calls the old workstation “the café,” while visiting crews
label it 日本語 on their diagrams. Both names—and the symbols 🚀 and 𝌆—must survive
the publishing path unchanged.

## What changes tonight

The release teaches the dashboard to group delayed readings by harbor entrance.
It also makes the offline banner quieter and gives operators a direct link to the
[tide-source runbook][runbook]. No sensor firmware changes during this window.

| Area | Change | Owner | Risk |
| :--- | :----- | :---- | ---: |
| Map | Cluster stale buoys | Mina | Low |
| Importer | Preserve source timestamps | Osei | Medium |
| Kiosk | Explain offline cache age | Ren | Low |

[runbook]: https://example.test/operations/tide-source "Tide source operations"

> A green map is not proof of fresh water-level data.
> Read the timestamp, then compare one buoy with the harbor master’s board.

### Before the window

- [x] Ask the harbor desk about dredging or channel closures.
- [x] Save a screenshot of the current entrance markers.
- [ ] Confirm the mirror has release tag `v4.8.0`.
- [ ] Put the rollback operator in the call.
  - Keep one person free to write the incident log.

The short command sequence is intentionally uneventful:

```shell
git fetch --tags origin
git verify-tag v4.8.0
./scripts/release-check --environment harbor
```

The verifier reads this compact plan:

```yaml
environment: harbor
release: 4.8.0
checks:
  - importer-lag
  - kiosk-cache
rollback_after_minutes: 12
```

<Callout tone="warning" title="Pause on missing observations">
  If two adjacent buoys are stale, contact {contacts[1]} before promoting.
  A single stale buoy can be isolated; a pair may indicate an upstream outage.
</Callout>

## Reading the evidence

The dashboard summary is useful, but the raw response explains why an entrance
changed color. Capture one sample before promotion and another afterward.

```json
{
  "station": "north-pier",
  "observedAt": "2031-04-09T21:24:00Z",
  "heightMeters": 2.14,
  "quality": "verified"
}
```

The browser adapter narrows the response before rendering it:

```typescript
type Reading = { station: string; observedAt: string; heightMeters: number };

export function ageInMinutes(reading: Reading, now: Date): number {
  const observed = Date.parse(reading.observedAt);
  return Math.floor((now.getTime() - observed) / 60_000);
}
```

{/* The status map is interactive in the published operations handbook. */}

<StatusMap
  harbor="north"
  release={release.version}
  stations={['north-pier', 'breakwater', 'old-ferry']}
  showFreshness
/>

### A small visual check

<Figure src="./harbor-entrances.png" alt="Three marked harbor entrances">
  Expected markers after promotion. Compare the image with the
  [live map](https://example.test/harbor/map?mode=operator&amp;layer=freshness).
</Figure>

1. Open the north-pier marker.
2. Read the observation time aloud.
3. Move to the breakwater marker and compare its age.

Do not mistake `receivedAt` for `observedAt`. The former can be recent even when
the sensor payload is old. The escaped form \{station\} in this paragraph is
documentation, not an interpolation, and &lt;unknown&gt; is an entity example.

---

## Promotion

At {release.window}, the release operator announces the version and the observer
answers with the latest importer lag. Promotion proceeds only when both values
match the notebook.

The service query used by the smoke check is similarly modest:

```graphql
query HarborFreshness($harbor: ID!) {
  harbor(id: $harbor) {
    stations { id freshness observedAt }
  }
}
```

<Tabs defaultValue="operator">
  <Tab value="operator" label="Operator">
    Run the promotion, watch importer lag, and keep the deployment event open.
  </Tab>
  <Tab value="observer" label="Observer">
    Compare the map with one raw reading and narrate unexpected movement.
  </Tab>
</Tabs>

<>
  <Callout tone="info" title="Two-minute observation">
    Normal queue movement can briefly reorder stations. Watch two refreshes before
    treating a changed row as a regression.
  </Callout>
  <p data-stage="promotion">Current target: {release.name}</p>
</>

## Rollback boundary

Rollback if the importer remains more than five minutes behind for two checks, if
the kiosk cannot load its cached chart, or if any station moves to the wrong harbor.
Do not rollback for a typo in the release note; fix the note separately.

The worker’s rollback state is deliberately tiny:

```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)]
enum ReleaseState {
    Observing,
    Promoted,
    RolledBack,
}
```

<Checklist
  title="Rollback handoff"
  items={['announce decision', 'restore prior image', 'verify one station']}
  owner={contacts.at(-1)}
/>

### Notes for the incident log

Use plain statements with times, for example: “21:36 UTC — north-pier fresh; old
ferry two minutes behind.” Avoid ~~guessing~~ conclusions until the raw reading and
import log agree. Mention @field-ops only when someone there can take an action.

For a forced line break, end the instruction here.\
Then put the observed result on its own line.

The literal characters \* \_ and \# are test data, while `a * b` remains inline
code. Named entities such as &copy; and &amp; should tokenize beside numeric &#9731;
without changing the surrounding prose.

## Closing the window

Departure checklist
-------------------

1. Confirm all three entrance markers show an observation age.
2. Close the temporary deployment event.
3. Add the release thread to the weekly operations digest.
4. Thank the harbor desk and return the radio.

![A quiet harbor after the evening check][harbor-photo]

[harbor-photo]: ./quiet-harbor.jpg "Harbor after sunset"

[^timing]: All times in this guide use UTC, even when the wall clock shows local time.

The window is complete when the index links to the evidence, not merely when the
deployment tool says “done.” Keep the final note short, factual, and linked.[^timing]
